title: 草かんむりの漢字
copy: 辞書上の部首が艸に分類されるJIS第一・第二水準の漢字。

items.name: Mallow
items.meaning: Mallow, hollyhock, or hibiscus; also used for several plant names.
items.note: Also associated with the aoi mon crest and the Tokugawa shogunate symbol.

items.name: Madder
items.meaning: Madder; also the reddish color or dye made from madder roots.

items.name: Common Reed
items.meaning: Common reed, especially Phragmites australis.
items.note: Also written 芦, 蘆, or 葭; よし is an alternate Japanese reading for the same plant.

items.name: Reed
items.meaning: Reed or bulrush; also used for common reed.

items.name: Wilt
items.meaning: To wither, wilt, droop, weaken, or decline.
items.note: Often appears in compounds such as 萎縮, withering or atrophy, and 萎靡, decline or decay.

items.name: Briar
items.meaning: Briar, bramble, thorny bush, or wild rose.
items.note: Also used figuratively for suffering, hardship, or distress.

items.name: Tuber
items.meaning: Edible tuber such as potato, sweet potato, yam, or taro.

items.name: Shade
items.meaning: Shade, shelter, or protection.
items.note: Closely related to 陰, another character for shade or shadow.

items.name: Artemisia
items.meaning: Artemisia japonica; also lush or luxuriant vegetation in literary usage.
items.note: The Japanese kun reading points to a mugwort-like Artemisia species; Chinese senses also include lush or magnificent growth.

items.name: Perilla
items.meaning: Perilla, especially Perilla frutescens; also soft or weak in older Chinese senses.
items.note: Associated with perilla oil in the etymology discussion.

items.name: English
items.meaning: Britain or English; also outstanding.

items.name: Amur Silvergrass
items.meaning: Amur silvergrass; also reed or cane.
items.note: Not to be confused with 萩 (hagi, bush clover).

items.name: Flower
items.meaning: Flower; a basic kanji for blossoms and flowers.

items.name: Harsh
items.meaning: Harsh or severe; also to bully or torment.

items.name: Eggplant
items.meaning: Eggplant or aubergine.

items.name: Load
items.meaning: Load, cargo, or luggage; also to shoulder or carry.

items.name: Confectionery
items.meaning: Confectionery or fruit; the kanji used in words such as 菓子.

items.name: Bud
items.meaning: Bud, sprout, or shoot; also the germ or beginning of an idea.

items.name: Mustard
items.meaning: Mustard; also rubbish, trash, or dust.

items.name: Kudzu
items.meaning: Kudzu or Japanese arrowroot; also creepers, wickerwork, kudzu starch, or cloth made from kudzu fiber.
items.note: The word kudzu is borrowed into English from Japanese.

items.name: Cattail
items.meaning: Cattail, bulrush, or reed; also short for calamus in compounds such as 菖蒲.
items.note: The plant name is often written in katakana as ガマ in biological contexts.

items.name: Thatch
items.meaning: Thatch or cogon grass.

items.name: Daylily
items.meaning: Daylily, especially Hemerocallis flava; in Japanese also thatching grass as an alternate spelling of 茅.
items.note: Also appears in personal names; 萱草 is read kanzo in Japanese compounds.

items.name: Mow
items.meaning: To cut, reap, or mow; also a sickle.

items.name: Water Dropwort
items.meaning: Water dropwort, an edible plant also counted among the seven spring herbs.

items.name: Bitter
items.meaning: Bitter; also pain, suffering, or hardship.

items.name: Art
items.meaning: Art, technique, craft, or performance; also rue as an older herb sense.

items.name: Greens
items.meaning: Greens, vegetables, rapeseed, or side dish.

items.name: Sowing
items.meaning: Sowing seeds; to sow, plant, or grow.

items.name: Turf
items.meaning: Turf, lawn, or grass.
items.note: In Chinese, 芝 is also associated with lingzhi or Ganoderma; the Japanese everyday sense is turf or lawn grass.

items.name: Young
items.meaning: Young; also used for conditional meanings such as if.

items.name: Iris
items.meaning: Iris, sweet flag, or calamus; mostly seen in compounds such as 菖蒲.

items.name: Core
items.meaning: Core, heart, wick, or pencil lead.

items.name: Sedge
items.meaning: Sedge, a grass-like plant; also used in surnames such as Suga or Kan.

items.name: Grass
items.meaning: Grass; also weed or herb in Japanese.

items.name: Moss
items.meaning: Moss.

items.name: Writing
items.meaning: To write or publish; also remarkable, renowned, or conspicuous.

items.name: Bush Clover
items.meaning: Bush clover, especially plants of the genus Lespedeza.
items.note: Culturally important as one of the seven flowers of autumn and as a hanafuda suit.

items.name: Seedling
items.meaning: Seedling or young plant.

items.name: Fragrant
items.meaning: Fragrant or sweet-smelling; also good reputation or satisfactory.

items.name: Sprout
items.meaning: Bud or sprout; also a sign of something beginning to emerge.
items.note: Also familiar in modern Japanese through 萌え, but the core kanji gloss is budding or sprouting.

items.name: Lotus
items.meaning: Lotus plant or flower, especially Nelumbo nucifera.
